message:
fix: replace legacy bd command references with br across codebase

The old Go beads CLI (bd) has been superseded by beads_rust (br), but
several user-facing strings still referenced the old binary name. This
caused agents and users to run non-existent commands.

Changes across 6 source files:
- cmd/bv/main.go: update claim/show commands in robot-next and
  robot-triage outputs from "bd update/show" to "br update/show"
- pkg/agents/blurb.go: update the AGENTS.md blurb template so
  generated documentation references br instead of bd for ready,
  list, show, create, update, close, and workflow instructions
- pkg/analysis/label_suggest.go: fix suggested action commands
  from "bd update" to "br update"
- pkg/analysis/triage.go: update command helpers (list-ready,
  list-blocked, claim, show, close, insight commands) to use br
- pkg/export/markdown.go: update markdown export claim/show
  command strings from bd to br
- pkg/ui/tutorial_content.go: update interactive tutorial text
  to reference br commands throughout

Also syncs .beads/issues.jsonl with current tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

No optimization needed. Closing as won't_fix.","created_at":"2026-01-21T17:56:00Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3v9s","title":"Replace hardcoded bd references with br throughout","description":"bv still emits hardcoded bd (old Go binary) commands in robot output, triage recommendations, markdown exports, and emit-script mode. All should use br (beads_rust). This is a bug since bd is deprecated.

ALL AFFECTED LOCATIONS (17 sites across 4 files):

cmd/bv/main.go:
- Line 2779: robot-next ClaimCmd (bd update %s --status=in_progress)
- Line 2780: robot-next ShowCmd (bd show %s)
- Line 2997: emit-script claim comment
- Line 2999: emit-script show command
- Line 3007: emit-script quick actions (top pick)
- Line 3012: emit-script quick actions (all items)

pkg/analysis/triage.go:
- Line 964: buildCommands() listReady (bd ready)
- Line 965: buildCommands() listBlocked (bd blocked)
- Line 970: buildCommands() claimTop (bd update --status in_progress) [note: missing = sign]
- Line 971: buildCommands() showTop (bd show)
- Line 1571: TrackRecommendationGroup ClaimCommand (CI=1 bd update --status in_progress --json)
- Line 1631: LabelRecommendationGroup ClaimCommand

pkg/export/markdown.go:
- Line 390: bd show bulk command
- Line 396: bd update bulk command
- Lines 422,428,436: bd update/show per-issue
- Line 439: bd show final

pkg/analysis/label_suggest.go:
- Line 242: bd update --add-label suggestion

Implementation:

1. Create pkg/analysis/cli_detect.go:
   - BeadsCLI() string: auto-detect br vs bd on PATH via exec.LookPath(); prefer br; error if neither found
   - ClaimCommand(id string) string: returns "br update ID --claim" (atomic) when br, or "bd update ID --status=in_progress" when bd
   - ShowCommand(id string) string: returns "br show ID" or "bd show ID"
   - ListReady() string: returns "br ready" or "bd ready"
   - ListBlocked() string: returns "br blocked" or "bd blocked"
   - LabelCommand(id, label string) string: for add-label operations
   - Cache detection result in package-level var (detect once, use many times)

2. Replace all 17 hardcoded bd references with dynamic calls to cli_detect functions
3. Unify command format: all robot/CI commands use "CI=1 br update ID --claim --json" pattern
4. Shell-escape IDs in emit-script output (use existing shellEscape helper where present)
5. Update existing tests to validate both br and bd command formats

EDGE CASES:
- Neither br nor bd on PATH: BeadsCLI() returns "br" with warning log (reasonable default since br is current)
- Old br version without --claim flag: ClaimCommand falls back to "br update ID --status=in_progress --actor $USER"
- IDs containing shell special chars: escape with single quotes in emit-script mode
- Windows paths in emit-script: use forward slashes (br handles both)
- Empty recommendations: quick actions section still generates valid (but empty) script

TESTS (28+ required):

Unit Tests (pkg/analysis/cli_detect_test.go):
- test_beads_cli_prefers_br: when both br and bd on PATH, returns "br"
- test_beads_cli_fallback_bd: when only bd on PATH, returns "bd"
- test_beads_cli_neither: when neither on PATH, returns "br" (default)
- test_claim_command_br: format is "br update ID --claim"
- test_claim_command_bd: format is "bd update ID --status=in_progress"
- test_show_command_format: both br and bd variants correct
- test_list_ready_format: both br and bd variants
- test_list_blocked_format: both br and bd variants
- test_label_command_format: both br and bd variants
- test_shell_escape_ids: IDs with spaces, quotes, slashes escaped properly
- test_detection_cached: BeadsCLI() called 100x returns same result without 100 exec.LookPath calls

Integration Tests (existing test files updated):
- test_robot_next_uses_br: --robot-next JSON contains "br" not "bd"
- test_robot_triage_uses_br: --robot-triage commands use "br"
- test_emit_script_uses_br: --emit-script bash output uses "br"
- test_emit_script_claim_all_loop: "claim all" loop uses correct br format
- test_markdown_export_uses_br: markdown export commands use "br"
- test_track_recommendation_claim: TrackRecommendationGroup.ClaimCommand uses "br update --claim"
- test_label_recommendation_claim: LabelRecommendationGroup.ClaimCommand uses "br update --claim"
- test_label_suggest_uses_br: label suggestion actions use "br"

E2E Tests:
- test_robot_next_output_contract: full --robot-next output validates against JSON schema with br commands
- test_emit_script_executable: emitted bash script is valid bash (shellcheck passes)
- test_markdown_export_valid: exported markdown has correct br commands

Regression:
- All existing emit_script_test.go tests updated to accept br instead of bd
- All existing robot_contract_test.go tests updated (lines 473-476)

GitHub issue: beads_viewer PR #100 (closed, reimplemented independently)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:08:09.417151886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T21:10:09.057382516Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3v9s","depends_on_id":"bd-x1tm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T21:10:09.057268834Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":66,"issue_id":"bd-3v9s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COORDINATION NOTE: This bead touches the same robot output paths as bd-x1tm (Normalize robot output envelope + _meta, P2, in-progress). When implementing:
1. Check bd-x1tm status first — if merged, robot-next/triage output format may have changed
2. The ClaimCmd/ShowCmd fields in robot-next JSON are affected by BOTH beads
3. If bd-x1tm adds _meta envelope wrapper, cli_detect functions should generate commands inside the new envelope format
4. Coordinate: implement bd-3v9s AFTER bd-x1tm lands to avoid merge conflicts in cmd/bv/main.go lines 2779-3012

ADDITIONAL TEST LOGGING REQUIREMENTS:
All test assertions should include descriptive failure messages, e.g.:
  assert.Contains(t, output, "br update", "robot-next ClaimCmd should use br, not bd")
  assert.NotContains(t, output, "bd ", "no hardcoded bd commands should remain in output")

E2E test scripts should log:
  - Which binary was detected (br vs bd) and full path
  - Each replacement site verified (file:line)
  - Before/after command format for each site
  - Pass/fail summary with count: 'Verified 17/17 sites migrated from bd to br'","created_at":"2026-02-07T21:10:04Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-sfad","title":"Test: release notes fallback workflow correctness","description":"## What
Test the release-notes-fallback workflow (bd-t8dx) to verify it correctly populates empty releases.

## Test Plan

### Test 1: Workflow YAML validation
1. Run `gh workflow list -R Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer` to verify workflow is registered
2. Validate YAML syntax: `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(\".github/workflows/release-notes-fallback.yml\"))"` 
3. Verify trigger is `on: release: types: [created, edited]`
4. Log: workflow name, triggers, jobs

### Test 2: Idempotency marker test
1. Create a draft release with body containing "<!-- auto-generated -->"
2. Trigger workflow (or simulate the logic)
3. Verify: body is NOT overwritten (idempotency marker detected)
4. Delete draft release
5. Log: release body before/after

### Test 3: Empty release gets notes
1. Create a draft release with empty body
2. Run the generate-notes API: `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/generate-notes -f tag_name=v0.14.4`
3. Verify: non-empty notes returned
4. Delete draft release
5. Log: generated notes content, character count

### Test 4: Existing notes are preserved
1. Create a draft release with body "Manual release notes here"
2. Verify: workflow would NOT overwrite (body length > 50 chars)
3. Delete draft release
4. Log: body content, skip reason

## Acceptance Criteria
- YAML is valid and workflow registers
- Idempotency prevents double-write
- Empty releases get auto-generated notes
- Manual releases are not overwritten","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:37:07.112634091Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:37:11.747019517Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer#99/test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","release","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sfad","depends_on_id":"bd-t8dx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:37:11.746934358Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-t8dx","title":"Add release notes fallback workflow for manual releases (GH #99)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #99 filed by qiviut (2026-02-06). Releases created by GoReleaser (via GH Actions) have full changelogs, but manually-created releases have no release notes. Example: v0.14.3 (GoReleaser) has notes, v0.14.4 (manual) has none.

## Owner Response
Confirmed the gap. When GoReleaser fails, binaries are uploaded manually via GitHub UI with minimal description. Proposed adding a fallback workflow triggered on release: [created] events.

## Current State
- Only workflow: .github/workflows/release.yml (triggers on push: tags: ['v*'], runs GoReleaser)
- GoReleaser config: .goreleaser.yaml (changelog: sort: asc, filters: exclude: ['^docs:', '^test:'])
- Beads bd-3v9s and bd-37ki created to track this, but NO actual workflow file was added
- Two commits (e0a1bd8, b50820a) only added beads entries, not implementation

## Implementation Plan
Create .github/workflows/release-notes-fallback.yml that:
1. Triggers on release: types: [created, edited]
2. Checks if release body is empty or minimal (< 50 characters)
3. Uses GitHub API: gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/generate-notes -f tag_name=$TAG
4. Updates release body: gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/releases/{id} -X PATCH -f body="$NOTES"
5. Includes idempotency marker (<!-- auto-generated -->) so it doesn't overwrite manually written notes on subsequent edits

## Key Files
- NEW: .github/workflows/release-notes-fallback.yml
- EXISTING: .github/workflows/release.yml (reference for trigger patterns)
- EXISTING: .goreleaser.yaml (reference for changelog config)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:17:19.887335054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T17:46:50.221771136Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T17:46:50.221749706Z","close_reason":"Enhanced release-notes.yml with edited trigger, 50-char threshold, idempotency marker, previous tag detection, and API-based note generation with fallback.","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/beads_viewer#99","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","release","workflow"],"comments":[{"id":67,"issue_id":"bd-t8dx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implementation: Create .github/workflows/release-notes-fallback.yml triggered on release created/edited. Step 1: check if body has idempotency marker or >50 chars (skip if so). Step 2: generate notes via gh api repos/OWNER/REPO/releases/generate-notes, append marker, PATCH release. Key: idempotency via HTML comment, 50-char threshold preserves manual notes, previous tag detection for accurate diff. See bd-sfad for test plan.","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:39:10Z"}]}

-{"id":"bd-x1tm","title":"TASK: Normalize robot output envelope + _meta","description":"Problem: robot outputs vary in metadata and envelope fields, making automation brittle.

Goal:
- Standardize a top-level envelope across all robot commands.
- Required fields: generated_at, data_hash, output_format, version; optional _meta with timings/cache/phase2_ready.
- Ensure errors are structured and consistent across commands.

Acceptance:
- All robot outputs share the same envelope keys.
- README/robot-docs updated with envelope spec.
- New tests or golden fixtures validate envelope consistency.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T10:10:09.421034701Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T17:50:00.260141464Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agent-friendly","consistency","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-x1tm","depends_on_id":"bd-c5g","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-25T10:10:09.421034701Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-x1tm","title":"TASK: Normalize robot output envelope + _meta","description":"Problem: robot outputs vary in metadata and envelope fields, making automation brittle.

}

 {"id":"bv-03h1","title":"Worker Health Monitoring and Watchdog Recovery","description":"## PURPOSE
Detect when the BackgroundWorker goroutine has died or become unresponsive,
and automatically recover. The UI should never become permanently stale due
to a silent worker failure.

## PROBLEM
Goroutines can fail silently:
- Panic not caught by recover()
- Deadlock on channel operation
- Infinite loop in processing
- Runtime crash in C code (rare but possible)

If the worker dies:
- File changes are no longer detected
- UI shows increasingly stale data
- User has no indication anything is wrong
- Only fix is to restart bv

## RATIONALE
Production systems need watchdogs. The UI depends on the worker goroutine
being alive. We must detect failure and recover automatically.

## SOLUTION

### 1. Heartbeat Mechanism

```go
type BackgroundWorker struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    
    heartbeatCh chan struct{}     // Worker sends heartbeat
    lastHeartbeat time.Time       // Last received heartbeat
    watchdogCtx   context.Context
    watchdogCancel context.CancelFunc
}

// Worker sends heartbeat periodically
func (w *BackgroundWorker) run() {
    defer w.wg.Done()
    
    heartbeatTicker := time.NewTicker(5 * time.Second)
    defer heartbeatTicker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-w.ctx.Done():
            return
            
        case <-w.watcher.Changed():
            w.handleFileChange()
            
        case <-heartbeatTicker.C:
            // Send heartbeat
            select {
            case w.heartbeatCh <- struct{}{}:
            default:
                // Channel full, that's fine
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### 2. Watchdog Goroutine

```go
// startWatchdog monitors worker health and restarts if needed
func (w *BackgroundWorker) startWatchdog() {
    w.watchdogCtx, w.watchdogCancel = context.WithCancel(context.Background())
    
    go func() {
        ticker := time.NewTicker(10 * time.Second)
        defer ticker.Stop()
        
        for {
            select {
            case <-w.watchdogCtx.Done():
                return
                
            case <-w.heartbeatCh:
                w.lastHeartbeat = time.Now()
                
            case <-ticker.C:
                w.checkHealth()
            }
        }
    }()
}

func (w *BackgroundWorker) checkHealth() {
    if time.Since(w.lastHeartbeat) > 30*time.Second {
        log.Printf("Worker heartbeat missed for 30s, attempting recovery")
        w.attemptRecovery()
    }
}
```

### 3. Recovery Logic

```go
func (w *BackgroundWorker) attemptRecovery() {
    // Increment recovery counter
    w.recoveryCount++
    if w.recoveryCount > 3 {
        log.Printf("Worker failed to recover after 3 attempts")
        // Send error to UI
        select {
        case w.errorCh <- SnapshotErrorMsg{
            Err:         errors.New("background worker unresponsive"),
            Recoverable: false,
        }:
        default:
        }
        return
    }
    
    // Cancel old context
    w.cancel()
    
    // Wait briefly for cleanup
    done := make(chan struct{})
    go func() {
        w.wg.Wait()
        close(done)
    }()
    
    select {
    case <-done:
        // Clean exit
    case <-time.After(5 * time.Second):
        log.Printf("Worker didn't exit cleanly, forcing restart")
    }
    
    // Restart
    w.ctx, w.cancel = context.WithCancel(context.Background())
    w.wg.Add(1)
    go w.run()
    
    // Trigger refresh
    w.triggerProcessing()
    
    log.Printf("Worker recovered (attempt %d)", w.recoveryCount)
}
```

### 4. Health Status Exposure

```go
type WorkerHealth struct {
    Alive         bool
    LastHeartbeat time.Time
    RecoveryCount int
    UptimeSince   time.Time
}

func (w *BackgroundWorker) Health() WorkerHealth {
    return WorkerHealth{
        Alive:         w.started.Load() && time.Since(w.lastHeartbeat) < 30*time.Second,
        LastHeartbeat: w.lastHeartbeat,
        RecoveryCount: w.recoveryCount,
        UptimeSince:   w.startTime,
    }
}
```

### 5. UI Health Indicator

```go
func (m Model) renderHealthIndicator() string {
    if m.backgroundWorker == nil {
        return ""
    }
    
    health := m.backgroundWorker.Health()
    if !health.Alive {
        return lipgloss.NewStyle().
            Foreground(lipgloss.Color("196")).
            Render("⚠ Worker unresponsive")
    }
    
    if health.RecoveryCount > 0 {
        return lipgloss.NewStyle().
            Foreground(lipgloss.Color("214")).
            Render(fmt.Sprintf("↻ Recovered x%d", health.RecoveryCount))
    }
    
    return ""
}
```

### 6. Panic Recovery in Worker

```go
func (w *BackgroundWorker) run() {
    defer w.wg.Done()
    defer func() {
        if r := recover(); r != nil {
            log.Printf("Worker panic recovered: %v\n%s", r, debug.Stack())
            // Watchdog will detect missed heartbeats and restart
        }
    }()
    
    // ... main loop
}
```

## TESTING

```go
func TestWatchdog_DetectsDeadWorker(t *testing.T) {
    worker := createTestWorker(t)
    worker.Start()
    
    // Simulate worker death by stopping heartbeats
    worker.stopHeartbeats()
    
    // Wait for watchdog to detect
    time.Sleep(35 * time.Second)
    
    // Should have attempted recovery
    require.Greater(t, worker.recoveryCount, 0)
}

func TestWatchdog_RecoversPanickedWorker(t *testing.T) {
    worker := createTestWorker(t)
    worker.Start()
    
    // Get initial snapshot
    <-worker.snapshotCh
    
    // Inject panic on next processing
    worker.injectPanic = true
    worker.TriggerRefresh()
    
    // Wait for recovery
    time.Sleep(35 * time.Second)
    
    // Should be alive again
    health := worker.Health()
    require.True(t, health.Alive)
    require.Equal(t, 1, health.RecoveryCount)
}

func TestWatchdog_GivesUpAfterMaxRetries(t *testing.T) {
    worker := createTestWorkerWithBrokenRun(t)
    worker.Start()
    
    // Wait for all recovery attempts
    time.Sleep(2 * time.Minute)
    
    // Should have given up
    require.Equal(t, 3, worker.recoveryCount)
    
    // Should have sent unrecoverable error
    err := <-worker.errorCh
    require.False(t, err.Recoverable)
}

func TestWatchdog_HeartbeatResetAfterRecovery(t *testing.T) {
    worker := createTestWorker(t)
    worker.Start()
    
    initialHeartbeat := worker.lastHeartbeat
    time.Sleep(6 * time.Second)
    
    // Heartbeat should have updated
    require.True(t, worker.lastHeartbeat.After(initialHeartbeat))
}
```

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Worker sends heartbeat every 5 seconds
- [ ] Watchdog checks health every 10 seconds
- [ ] Recovery triggered after 30 seconds no heartbeat
- [ ] Max 3 recovery attempts before giving up
- [ ] Panic in worker caught and recovered
- [ ] Health status exposed to UI
- [ ] UI shows indicator when worker unhealthy
- [ ] Tests verify detection and recovery
